meta_title: Roofing in Pembroke Pines, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Pembroke Pines, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates & financing from $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing in Pembroke Pines, FL — Built for South Florida's Toughest Storms
intro:
Pembroke Pines is one of Broward County's most established and beloved communities, home to tens of thousands of families spread across thriving neighborhoods like Chapel Trail, Silver Lakes, Pembroke Falls, Pembroke Isles, and Century Village. With average home values sitting around $420,000, your roof isn't just a structural necessity — it's one of the most important investments protecting that value. Whether you live in a newer build near Chapel Trail Nature Preserve or a well-established home closer to Pembroke Lakes Mall, the condition of your roof directly determines how well your home withstands South Florida's relentless heat, humidity, and hurricane season. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand exactly what Pembroke Pines homes demand, and we deliver roofing systems built to exceed those demands.

Pembroke Pines sits roughly 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, placing it squarely in Broward County's high-wind design territory with a rated wind zone of 170 mph. While the city falls just outside Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), that distinction doesn't reduce your exposure to storm-force winds, wind-driven rain, or flying debris during major weather events. In fact, approximately 35% of homes in Pembroke Pines were built before 1994 — meaning they predate the sweeping post-Hurricane Andrew building code reforms. Many of those roofs were installed under standards that are now considered wholly inadequate, making a modern replacement not just a cosmetic upgrade but a critical safety measure.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in full roof replacements and targeted repairs using hurricane-rated systems engineered for conditions just like what Pembroke Pines experiences. From architectural shingles to concrete and clay tile roofing, we bring 60+ years of combined installation experience to every project in this city. We've completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we know the specific permitting process, code requirements, and roofing challenges unique to this community. When you work with us, you're not hiring a contractor who's learning on the job — you're hiring the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Pembroke Pines Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof
section_why.body:

We are a fully licensed and insured roofing company with an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau — a reflection of our commitment to doing right by every homeowner, every time. When you call us, you're not handed off to a subcontractor. Every roof installation and repair we complete is performed by our own in-house crews, trained to our exacting standards and accountable to our name.

That distinction matters more than most homeowners realize. Many roofing companies in South Florida rely heavily on third-party labor, which means inconsistent quality, miscommunication, and zero accountability when something goes wrong. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, our team shows up, does the work, and stands behind it. Our 60+ years of combined experience across our installation teams means we've seen every roof type, every damage pattern, and every challenge that Pembroke Pines homes can throw at us — from the clay tile rooftops of Pembroke Isles to the shingle-clad homes of Silver Lakes and the manufactured-home communities near Century Village.

We also understand the local permitting environment inside and out. Our team is fully familiar with the City of Pembroke Pines Building Division and its specific inspection and documentation requirements. We handle every permit, every inspection, and all necessary wind mitigation reporting on your behalf. You won't have to chase down paperwork or navigate bureaucratic delays — we manage the entire process so your project moves forward smoothly and is completed right the first time.

Financing is another area where we go the extra mile for Pembroke Pines homeowners. We know that a full roof replacement is a significant investment, which is why we offer financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. A quality roof shouldn't be out of reach for middle-income families in this community, and we've structured our financing options to make that a reality. From initial estimate to final inspection, the Premier Impact Windows & Roofing experience is built around you.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Shaped Pembroke Pines — And Why Your Roof Must Be Ready
section_history.body:
Pembroke Pines has experienced no fewer than 9 named storms since 1990, and the scars of those events are still visible on older rooftops across the city. The most consequential chapter in local storm history begins with Hurricane Andrew in August 1992. Though Andrew made landfall further south near Homestead, its outer bands and surge of sustained winds battered Broward County, exposing serious vulnerabilities in the roofing systems of the era. Homes throughout Pembroke Pines suffered significant damage, and the event triggered a wholesale overhaul of Florida's building codes. Unfortunately, many homes built before that watershed moment still carry aging roofs that were never brought up to post-Andrew standards.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered another harsh reminder of what South Florida wind can do. As one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, Wilma moved across Broward County with surprising speed, which actually intensified its destructive impact. The rapid pressure changes and sustained winds caused widespread roof damage in Pembroke Pines neighborhoods, with shingles torn off, tiles displaced, and underlayment compromised across thousands of homes. Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017, which brought sustained tropical-storm-force winds and heavy rain that tested even relatively newer roofing systems. Properties near C.B. Smith Park and throughout the western reaches of Chapel Trail experienced notable roof stress during Irma's passage.

This history isn't just interesting — it's a practical warning. A roof that has survived multiple major storms without replacement may be operating on borrowed time. Hidden damage to underlayment, damaged fasteners, and compromised flashing don't always announce themselves with obvious leaks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's roofing teams are trained to conduct thorough assessments that reveal what the naked eye often misses, giving Pembroke Pines homeowners a clear picture of their roof's actual condition and what it will take to ensure they're fully protected heading into the next hurricane season.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Roofing Requirements in Pembroke Pines
section_code.body:
Roofing in Pembroke Pines is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), Eighth Edition, with Broward County local amendments that establish some of the most rigorous construction standards in the country. While Pembroke Pines does not fall within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation typically reserved for Miami-Dade County — the city still sits within a 170 mph wind design zone, which mandates hurricane-rated roofing products, approved installation methods, and specific fastening schedules that differ meaningfully from what is required in lower-wind regions of Florida.

All roofing materials installed in Pembroke Pines must carry Florida Product Approvals (NOA or FL#) and be installed in strict accordance with manufacturer specifications and code requirements. This includes minimum nail penetration depths, underlayment specifications (typically a self-adhering modified bitumen or two layers of 30-lb felt installed per code), and deck attachment requirements. For tile roofing, specific mortar or foam adhesive standards apply depending on the pitch and exposure category of the structure. Non-compliant installation can void your manufacturer warranty, expose you to insurance claim denials, and potentially require costly tear-out and re-installation.

Permits for all roofing work in Pembroke Pines are issued through the City of Pembroke Pines Building Division, located at 10100 Pines Boulevard. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on behalf of our clients — from application submission to scheduling inspections and obtaining the final signed-off certificate of completion. Our team is intimately familiar with the documentation requirements of the Pembroke Pines Building Division and knows how to navigate the process efficiently. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, your project is code-compliant from day one, fully permitted, and backed by inspection records you can present to your insurance carrier or a future home buyer with complete confidence.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile — Premium Roofing for Pembroke Pines Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing product lines that we've selected specifically for their performance in South Florida's demanding environment: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both brands are Florida-approved, hurricane-rated, and engineered to handle the wind speeds, UV exposure, and humidity conditions that Pembroke Pines homeowners face year-round.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles are among the most popular and trusted shingle products in the Florida market, and for good reason. The Landmark Series features a dual-layer construction that delivers exceptional wind resistance — rated up to 110 mph with standard installation, and eligible for enhanced wind warranty coverage when installed with specific fastening patterns. The SureStart PLUS limited warranty provides coverage for the life of the shingle on the original structure, and the product's Algae Resistance (AR) technology is particularly important in Pembroke Pines, where humidity and organic growth can rapidly discolor and degrade lesser shingles. CertainTeed shingles are available in dozens of color profiles that complement both the newer Mediterranean-style homes common to Pembroke Falls and the traditional ranch-style homes found throughout older sections of the city.

For homeowners seeking the classic South Florida aesthetic of tile roofing — or looking to replace an existing clay or concrete tile system — West Lake's concrete and clay tile lines deliver outstanding durability and code compliance. West Lake tiles are Florida Product Approved and designed specifically for high-wind environments, with tested resistance to wind uplift forces consistent with Broward County's design requirements. Concrete tile offers exceptional longevity, often lasting 40 to 50 years when properly installed, while clay tile provides a distinctive look and slightly lighter weight profile suited to certain structure types. Both options offer superior thermal performance compared to standard shingles, helping to reduce cooling loads and energy costs in Pembroke Pines homes during the long, hot South Florida summer.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Costs in Pembroke Pines: What to Expect and How to Finance It
section_cost.body:
The cost of a roof replacement in Pembroke Pines varies based on the size of your home, the roofing material selected, the condition of the existing deck, and the complexity of your roof's design. For a typical single-family home in this market — where the average home value sits around $420,000 — a full shingle roof replacement generally ranges from $12,000 to $22,000, while a tile roof replacement can range from $20,000 to $40,000 or more depending on tile type and structural requirements. These figures include permitting, disposal of the old roof system, new underlayment, and all labor performed by our in-house crews.

When evaluating the cost of a new roof, it's important to consider the return on investment rather than looking at the price tag in isolation. According to Remodeling Magazine's Cost vs. Value Report, a new roof consistently ranks among the highest-ROI home improvements a homeowner can make, often recovering 60 to 70 percent of its cost at resale — and in a competitive market like Broward County, a fresh, code-compliant roof can be the difference between a quick sale at full asking price and a prolonged negotiation. Add in the insurance savings available to Pembroke Pines homeowners after a qualifying roof replacement (discussed in the next section), and the true net cost of your investment looks considerably more manageable.

To make this essential upgrade accessible to every homeowner in our community, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available to qualified applicants. Our financing partners offer fast approvals and competitive terms, so you don't have to delay a project that is protecting your family and your most valuable asset. We'll walk you through every financing option during your free estimate appointment so you can make an informed decision that fits your household budget.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Benefits and Wind Mitigation Savings for Pembroke Pines Homeowners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for a new roof in Pembroke Pines is the impact it can have on your homeowners insurance premium. Florida insurance carriers are required by law to offer discounts to policyholders who can demonstrate that their home has qualifying wind mitigation features — and a new, hurricane-rated roof is one of the most powerful wind mitigation upgrades you can make. Following a qualifying roof replacement, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can provide documentation to support a wind mitigation inspection, which is submitted to your insurer as the basis for recalculating your premium.

The specific discounts available depend on the roof covering type, attachment method, roof deck attachment, and whether your home has a hip roof configuration — all factors that are evaluated during a wind mitigation inspection. In Broward County, homeowners who switch to a hurricane-rated shingle or tile system with a sealed roof deck (using self-adhering underlayment) can see insurance premium reductions ranging from 15% to 45% or more, depending on their current policy structure and carrier. For a homeowner paying $4,000 to $8,000 per year in wind insurance premiums — which is not uncommon in the current Florida market — that represents hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual savings.

Many major insurance carriers active in the Pembroke Pines and greater Broward County market, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance, recognize wind mitigation improvements and adjust premiums accordingly. Some carriers also require proof of a compliant, up-to-code roof in order to renew or maintain coverage altogether — making a roof replacement not just financially advantageous but potentially necessary to keep your home insurable. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team is experienced in providing the documentation, product approvals, and installation records you'll need to maximize your insurance benefits after your new roof is installed.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Pembroke Pines home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on several factors: the age of your current roof, the extent and type of damage, and whether your existing system meets current Florida Building Code standards. Homes in Pembroke Pines built before 1994 are especially likely to have roofing systems that are both aging and non-compliant with post-Hurricane Andrew codes. If your roof is more than 15 to 20 years old, shows widespread shingle granule loss, has multiple areas of soft decking, or has experienced recurring leaks, a full replacement is typically more cost-effective and safer than continued patchwork repair. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections and will give you an honest assessment.

faqs.q: How much does a new roof cost in Pembroke Pines, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: In Pembroke Pines, a full shingle roof replacement typically runs between $12,000 and $22,000, while tile replacements range from $20,000 to $40,000 or more depending on tile type and home size. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified homeowners. Our financing process is fast and straightforward, and we'll walk you through your options during your free estimate. When combined with potential insurance savings post-installation, the net investment is often significantly lower than the sticker price suggests.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Pembroke Pines, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements — and most significant repairs — in Pembroke Pines. Permits are issued through the City of Pembroke Pines Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process for you, including application submission, scheduling required inspections, and obtaining the final certificate of completion. We're thoroughly familiar with the Building Division's documentation requirements and ensure that all work is performed to code, giving you a fully permitted, inspection-approved roof with proper records for insurance and future resale.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Pembroke Pines?
faqs.a: For most standard single-family homes in Pembroke Pines, a full shingle roof replacement can be completed in one to two days once materials are on-site. Tile roof replacements typically take two to four days due to the additional complexity of installation. The overall timeline from contract signing to project completion — including permitting — generally ranges from two to four weeks, depending on permit turnaround times and material availability. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed throughout the process and works efficiently to minimize disruption to your daily routine.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in Pembroke Pines?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — and often substantially. Florida law requires carriers to offer discounts for homes with qualifying wind mitigation features, and a new hurricane-rated roof is among the most impactful upgrades you can make. After installation, we provide the product approval documents and installation records needed for a wind mitigation inspection. Broward County homeowners with qualifying roof systems, including sealed roof decks and approved coverings, commonly see premium reductions of 15% to 45%. Given current insurance rates in the Pembroke Pines area, that can translate to hundreds of dollars in annual savings.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roofing estimate — our in-house experts are ready to evaluate your home and deliver the Kings of Service experience that Pembroke Pines homeowners trust.
